title: Battery module including a heat pipe positioned in proximity to a terminal component at a positive or negative terminal of the battery module
abstract: A battery module, including a plurality of battery cell groups that are connected in series with each other, each of the plurality of battery cell groups including a plurality of battery cells that are connected to each other in parallel, a first terminal component at a first terminal of the battery module, the first terminal corresponding to either a positive terminal of the battery module or a negative terminal of the battery module, and a first heat pipe positioned in proximity to the first terminal component and configured to transfer heat away from the first terminal component.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A battery module, comprising: a plurality of battery cell groups that are connected in series with each other, each of the plurality of battery cell groups including a plurality of battery cells that are connected to each other in parallel; a first terminal component at a first terminal of the battery module, the first terminal corresponding to either a positive terminal of the battery module or a negative terminal of the battery module; and a first heat pipe positioned in proximity to the first terminal component and configured to transfer heat away from the first terminal component. 2. The battery module of claim 1, further comprising: a second terminal component at a second terminal of the battery module; and a second heat pipe positioned in proximity to the second terminal component and configured to transfer heat away from the second terminal component. 3. The battery module of claim 2, further comprising: a cooling plate that is configured to be cooled by an external liquid cooling system, wherein the first and second heat pipes are each directly or indirectly coupled to the cooling plate. 4. The battery module of claim 1, further comprising: a cooling plate that is configured to be cooled by an external liquid cooling system, wherein the first heat pipe is directly or indirectly coupled to the cooling plate. 5. The battery module of claim 1, further comprising: 

 a layer of thermally conductive and electrically insulative material arranged between the first terminal component and the first heat pipe, such that the first heat pipe and the first terminal component are not directly coupled, wherein the layer of thermally conductive and electrically insulative material facilitates heat to be transferred from the first terminal component to the first heat pipe while mitigating electricity from being transferred from the first terminal component to the first heat pipe. 6. The battery module of claim 5, wherein the layer of thermally conductive and electrically insulative material includes insulation foil. 7. The battery module of claim 6, wherein the insulation foil includes Kapton foil, ceramic insulation, anodized aluminum, or a combination thereof. 8. The battery module of claim 5, further comprising: a gap filler layer positioned between the layer of thermally conductive and electrically insulative material and the first heat pipe. 9. The battery module of claim 1, wherein the first terminal component is a positive or negative high-voltage (HV) connector of the battery module. 10. The battery module of claim 1, wherein the first terminal component is part of a contact plate that is configured with a set of bonding connectors that establish direct electrical bonds with positive or negative terminals of one of the plurality of battery cell groups. 1 1. The battery module of claim 10, wherein a portion of the contact plate protrudes outside the battery module, and wherein the first heat pipe is positioned in proximity to a second portion of the contact plate inside the battery module. 12. The battery module of claim 10, wherein a portion of the contact plate that protrudes outside the battery module is configured as a high-voltage (HV) connector for the battery module. 13. The battery module of claim 10, wherein the first heat pipe is positioned in proximity to a thickest portion of the contact plate that is associated with a highest current expectation across the contact plate.
